symptoms.symptom_name: Acute hot swollen single joint, non-weight-bearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute hot swollen non-weight-bearing joint is septic arthritis until excluded by aspiration.
Also fits an acute palindromic flare, but that cannot be assumed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid onset over 4 hours in a known palindromic pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid self-limiting flares are characteristic of palindromic rheumatism.
Afebrile lowers, but does not exclude, septic arthritis (early septic joints can be afebrile).
1st MTP sparing is somewhat atypical for gout.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person assessment with joint aspiration if indicated
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only way to exclude septic arthritis — cannot be done remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers, urate, blood cultures if febrile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the assessment of infection/crystal disease.
